subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The investigation clearly communicates its purpose and research question with appropriate headings and a logical chapter structure.
The methodology integrates a range of relevant techniques (electro-oxidation, mass measurement, frequency analysis) demonstrating informed selection.
weaknesses: Lengthy, anecdotal introduction occasionally dilutes focus and precision.
Key variables (tension, temperature, humidity) are not consistently controlled or justified, reducing methodological rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows an adequate but not consistently precise focus and method. The research question is clear and maintained, and the methodology is appropriate, but narrative digressions and incomplete control of confounding factors limit the sharpness and completeness required for a higher mark. A mid-band score of 3/6 reflects these strengths and shortcomings.

strengths: Relevant physics and chemistry concepts (frequency, mass per unit length, electrolysis) are introduced and linked to the investigation.
Sources are cited, indicating an attempt to ground discussion in subject knowledge.
weaknesses: Several conceptual inaccuracies (e.g., misinterpretation of the massâfrequency relationship) reveal uneven understanding.
Terminology and units are applied inconsistently, with mixing of languages and formatting errors disrupting precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge and understanding are competent but uneven. The student demonstrates awareness of relevant theories and sources, yet conceptual errors and inconsistent use of technical terms prevent authoritative treatment. A low mid-band score of 2/6 appropriately reflects this level of performance.

strengths: Primary data are collected, tabulated, and graphed, with qualitative interpretation of trends directly linked to the research question.
The report follows a coherent structure (introduction, method, analysis, conclusion), supporting the development of an argument.
weaknesses: Statistical treatment of uncertainties is minimal and lacks quantitative propagation of error.
Evaluation of results remains largely descriptive, with limited critical engagement to reconcile discrepancies between theory and experiment.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical thinking shows good research analysis and a clear, structured argument, but it falls short of sophisticated evaluation and rigorous uncertainty analysis. A mark of 4/12 in the lower mid-band accurately balances competent reasoning against the need for deeper critical evaluation.

strengths: The investigation is well structured, with a title page, table of contents, numbered pages, and labelled figures and tables.
Visual elements (photographs, graphs, diagrams) are generally relevant and aid comprehension.
weaknesses: Formatting of tables, figure captions, and references is inconsistent, with mixed styles and occasional cropping issues.
Some images lack proper lighting, scale bars, or clear annotations, reducing their clarity and professionalism.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is clear and functional, with a coherent layout and use of visual aids. However, inconsistent formatting and deficiencies in image quality prevent a top-band score. A mark of 3/4 reflects the overall readability while acknowledging areas for improvement.

strandSelections.reasoning: Technical terms such as âfrecuenciaâ, âmasa eficazâ, âtensiÃ³nâ, ânodoâ and âcelda electrolÃ­ticaâ are employed, yet definitions are sometimes imprecise and units are inconsistently written (e.g., commas instead of decimal points, Hz values mis-rounded).  Misapplication of the fundamental frequency equation reveals incomplete conceptual command.  Occasional mixing of Spanish and English terms (âPhyphoxâ, âfrequencyâ) disrupts precision.  While terminology is present, inconsistent accuracy and application limit this strand to the upper end of the 1-2 band.

An argument is developed that oxidation alters mass and therefore frequency; the student links experimental results to this claim.  However, the line of reasoning is frequently descriptive, and logical coherence suffers when the conclusion contradicts the theoretical prediction without rigorous justification.  Structure is present (introductionâmethodâanalysisâconclusion) but within sections the flow is disjointed and sometimes repetitive.  Overall, argumentation is limited but identifiable, placing it at the top of the 1-3 band.

The student describes a procedure that combines electro-oxidation, mass measurement, a home-made resonance box and smartphone frequency analysis.  This shows some informed selection of methods and a logical link to the question.  However, the methodology lacks completeness: key variables such as string tension, temperature and humidity are not properly controlled or monitored; sample size is very small; calibration and uncertainty treatment are cursory.  The explanation of how each step will generate valid data is only partial.  These shortcomings restrict reliability, placing the work in the lower half of the 3-4 band.

The student demonstrates a sound but uneven grasp of wave behaviour, stationary waves and basic electro-chemistry. Relevant concepts such as frequency, wavelength, tension and mass per unit length are introduced and linked to guitar strings.  Nevertheless, several conceptual inaccuracies appear (e.g., concluding that increased mass raises frequency, contrary to accepted theory) and the discussion of electro-plating/oxidation includes vague chemistry. Source material is referenced but not always integrated analytically.  Overall knowledge is competent rather than authoritative, consistent with the 3-4 band, lower end.

The student recognises several sources of error (tension variations, non-uniform oxidation, external noise) and acknowledges that these may explain unexpected results, showing some awareness of limitations.  Nonetheless, the evaluation remains surface-level; no quantitative assessment of uncertainties or suggestions for methodological improvement are offered beyond general remarks.  There is little critical engagement with literature to reconcile discrepancies between theory and findings.  This represents a basic, largely descriptive evaluation, appropriate to the middle of the 1-3 band.

The title and opening paragraph clearly identify the focus on âcÃ³mo la masa de Ã³xido afecta la frecuencia de la cuerda nÂ° 6 de la guitarra elÃ©ctricaâ, so the reader can understand immediately what is being investigated. Headings, a table of contents and explicit reference to âCapÃ­tulo 2: ExperimentaciÃ³nâ help convey purpose. However, lengthy narrative sentences, occasional digressions and some linguistic inaccuracy reduce precision and concision; the focus sometimes blurs when the student explains background anecdotes rather than the central problem. Overall, communication is adequate and generally clear, but not consistently sharp or concise enough for the top band.

Photographs, tables and graphs are included, generally relevant and captioned, aiding comprehension.  The homemade diagrams are legible and support explanations.  However, some images are redundant or poorly cropped, axis labels mix languages, and uncertainties are not always visible despite being claimed.  Spacing and alignment are occasionally uneven.  These inconsistencies prevent the highest achievement but the visual presentation is functional and clearly linked to the text, fitting the lower end of the 3-4 band.

The research question is explicitly stated, focused on one variable (mass of oxide) and one dependent variable (frequency of string nÂ° 6).  It is revisited in the hypothesis, data-processing table and conclusion, showing an adequate thread of inquiry.  Nonetheless, the experimental design does not maintain strict control of confounding factors (tension, humidity, non-uniform oxidation), so the operational focus weakens at times, and the analysis partially drifts into descriptive narrative.  Thus the question is clear and mostlyâbut not fullyâsustained, meriting the middle of the 3-4 band.

The student collects primary data (mass and frequency) and organises it in tables and a graph, offering a qualitative interpretation of trends and mentioning uncertainties.  The analysis identifies a positive correlation and comments on concavity, but statistical treatment is minimal and the physical explanation conflicts with established theory.  Evaluation of data reliability focuses mainly on obvious experimental errors (background noise, string breakage) with limited depth.  The work shows an adequate, though not consistently accurate, attempt at analysis, fitting the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The essay is within the word limit, contains a title page, table of contents, numbered pages, labelled figures and tables, and follows a recognisable chapter structure.  Citations and a reference list are provided, albeit with inconsistent formatting.  Minor issues such as mixed language use in headings and occasional typographic errors detract from full professionalism, but overall the structure is clear and facilitates reading, justifying placement in the lower half of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The lengthy, anecdotal introduction dilutes focus. It would benefit from being streamlined to foreground the physics context and research purpose.
The methodology lacks justification of key parameters (voltage, solution concentration, duration). Explain how each choice ensures valid mass changes.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly formatted, enhancing document navigation and overall structure.
The figure caption is minimal. Consistent formatting and descriptive captions (including key parameters) would improve clarity.
The electrolysis description uses vague or incorrect terminology (e.g., galvanoplastia). Clarify oxidation and reduction at each electrode with correct chemical language.
Failures during full-string oxidation are noted but not systematically recorded. Document each trialâs conditions and outcomes quantitatively.
The conceptual definition of waves is generic; it should be explicitly linked to guitar string physics to demonstrate deep understanding.
The discussion of stationary waves omits the fundamental frequency equation. Include $$f=\frac{1}{2L}\sqrt{\frac{T}{\mu}}$$ to link mass per unit length to frequency.
Proposed solutions (resonance box, salt intervals) lack theoretical justification or literature support. Anchor design changes in physics principles.
Tension control is critical but unexplained. Describe how string tension was measured, maintained, and checked between oxidations.
Table 1âs headers are misaligned and units are unclear. Align columns properly and include units in each column heading.
Ambient noise effects are noted but not controlled. Implement a sound-proofing method or baseline subtraction to isolate string frequency.
The title is clearly stated at the very start, establishing the investigationâs focus effectively and meeting the requirement for an explicit beginning.
A precise word count is provided at the outset, demonstrating adherence to the prescribed word-limit formatting requirement.
Figure 3âs caption is overly brief. Include details like voltage applied and exposure time to make the image self-explanatory.
The photograph in Figure 4 is under-lit and lacks labeling. Improved lighting and callouts would clarify the experimental setup components.
Averages are computed correctly but lack uncertainty propagation. Calculate the standard error or confidence interval for the mean frequency.
Figure 7 shows a trend but missing quantitative analysis. Fit a trend line, report slope, RÂ², and discuss statistical significance against theoretical prediction.
The figure caption refers to negative mass values that do not match the data set. Ensure axis labels and data points are consistent.
Figure 8 lacks a scale or reference object to gauge uniformity of oxidation. Include a ruler or scale bar for quantitative assessment.
The research question is clear but could be more sharply phrased and positioned. A succinct, standalone statement would strengthen operational focus.
The pre-test on a 7 cm string is unexplained in terms of replication and variability control. Specify sample size and statistical rationale.
Figure 9 would benefit from clearer focus and consistent background to highlight the break location. Improve contrast and framing.
Consistent page numbering in the footer aids readability and navigation throughout the document.
References are inconsistently styled (italics, retrieval dates). Apply a uniform citation format (e.g., APA) for professional presentation.
